description:
German Credit Data (Teeny-Tiny Castle)
	

This dataset is part of a tutorial tied to the Teeny-Tiny Castle, an open-source repository containing educational tools for AI Ethics and Safety research. 

	
		
		How to Use
	

from datasets import load_dataset

dataset = load_dataset("AiresPucrs/german-credit-data", split = 'train')

results.reason: Refers to a technical data split (e.g., train, test), not an IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristic.
This column indicates a dataset split and does not represent any IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristic.
Directly represents 'Age' from the 'Age' IN-SCOPE category.
Directly represents 'Sex' from the 'Gender & sexual identity' IN-SCOPE category.
Can be a strong proxy for 'Socioeconomic status: social origin/class'.
Strongly indicates 'Socioeconomic status: social origin/class' and 'property/ownership type'.
Strongly indicates 'Socioeconomic status: social origin/class' based on financial assets.
Strongly indicates 'Socioeconomic status: social origin/class' based on financial assets.
Strongly indicates 'Socioeconomic status: social origin/class' based on financial capacity and debt.
Refers to the duration of credit, which is not a direct or strong proxy for any IN-SCOPE category.
Can weakly correlate with 'Socioeconomic status' depending on the nature of the purpose.
Represents a credit risk assessment, which is not an IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristic of an individual.
